GROUND TRUTH — pmemd rejects irest=1 with ntx=1
================================================

This file records the REAL pmemd run that proves `irest=1, ntx=1` is genuinely
illegal (not a synthetic assumption). It is the ground truth behind the
`irest/ntx incoherent` FAIL gate in ../../checks/check_amber.py. The companion
fixture `restart_irest1_ntx1_illegal.in` is the exact namelist pmemd choked on;
the oracle test (../test_restart_coherence.py, Tier C) asserts the gate FAILs on
those exact bytes — so the gate and its test cannot both be wrong.

Induction (single variable)
---------------------------
Base namelist : project-prime/golden-path/prod.in  (a real GREEN production
                restart stage: imin=0, irest=1, ntx=5, reads equil.rst).
Change        : ONLY ntx flipped 5 -> 1 (irest=1 kept); nstlim trimmed 5000 -> 10
                for speed. Nothing else changed.
Topology      : project-prime/golden-path/system.prmtop
Restart (vel) : project-prime/golden-path/equil.rst  (NetCDF AMBERRESTART,
                carries coordinates + velocities + box)
Engine        : pmemd Version 26.0  (local build, ~/Downloads/pmemd26)
Date          : 2026-06-27

Command
-------
pmemd -O -i prod_ntx1_illegal.in -o illegal.mdout \
      -p system.prmtop -c equil.rst -r illegal.rst

Result — ILLEGAL (ntx=1, irest=1)
---------------------------------
pmemd mdout (verbatim):
    | ERROR:   ntx and irest are inconsistent!

     Input errors occurred. Terminating execution.
pmemd stderr:
    STOP PMEMD Terminated Abnormally!
exit code: 1

Control — identical namelist but ntx=5 (legal)
----------------------------------------------
Ran all 10 steps to completion. exit code: 0.
Because ntx is the ONLY difference between the two runs, it is unambiguously the
cause: pmemd itself names the `ntx and irest are inconsistent!` failure, which is
exactly the condition the gate fires on (imin=0, irest=1, ntx not in {4,5,6,7}).
